description.para: Offsets of registers from the start of the device
This register controls how the time out is indicated and also contains the access code (0xABC) to allow writes to the register
This register controls how fast the timer runs and the reset value and also contains the access code (0x248) to allow writes to the register
This register resets the timer preventing a timeout.
This register indicates timer reached zero count.

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each watchdog timer device in the system.
Initialize a specific watchdog timer instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para: This function must be called before other functions of the driver are called.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.
is the config structure.
is the base address for the device. It could be a virtual address if address translation is supported in the system, otherwise it is the physical address.

listitem.para: XST_DEVICE_IS_STARTED if the device has already been started.

briefdescription.para: Start the watchdog timer of the device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.

detaileddescription.para: It is the caller's responsibility to disconnect the interrupt handler of the watchdog timer from the interrupt source, typically an interrupt controller, and disable the interrupt in the interrupt controller.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.

detaileddescription.para: Performs a read/modify/write cycle to update the value correctly.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.
is the desired signal/output. Valid Signal Values are XWDTPS_RESET_SIGNAL and XWDTPS_IRQ_SIGNAL. Only one of them can be specified at a time.

detaileddescription.para: Performs a read/modify/write cycle to update the value correctly.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.
is the desired signal/output. Valid Signal Values are XWDTPS_RESET_SIGNAL and XWDTPS_IRQ_SIGNAL Only one of them can be specified at a time.

briefdescription.para: Returns the current control setting for the indicated signal/output.

detaileddescription.para: The register referenced is the Counter Control Register (XWDTPS_CCR_OFFSET)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.
is the desired signal/output. Valid Control Values are XWDTPS_CLK_PRESCALE and XWDTPS_COUNTER_RESET. Only one of them can be specified at a time.

simplesect.para: The contents of the requested control field in the Counter Control Register (XWDTPS_CCR_OFFSET). If the Control is XWDTPS_CLK_PRESCALE then use the definitions XWDTEPB_CCR_PSCALE_XXXX. If the Control is XWDTPS_COUNTER_RESET then the values are 0x0 to 0xFFF. This is the Counter Restart value in the CCR register.

briefdescription.para: Updates the current control setting for the indicated signal/output with the provided value.

detaileddescription.para: Performs a read/modify/write cycle to update the value correctly. The register referenced is the Counter Control Register (XWDTPS_CCR_OFFSET)

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.
is the desired signal/output. Valid Control Values are XWDTPS_CLK_PRESCALE and XWDTPS_COUNTER_RESET. Only one of them can be specified at a time.

is the desired control value. If the Control is XWDTPS_CLK_PRESCALE then use the definitions XWDTEPB_CCR_PSCALE_XXXX. If the Control is XWDTPS_COUNTER_RESET then the valid values are 0x0 to 0xFFF, this sets the counter restart value of the CCR register.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XWdtPs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xwdtps.h for the definition of XWdtPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: Run a self-test on the timebase.

detaileddescription.para: This test verifies that the register access locking functions. This is tested by trying to alter a register without setting the key value and verifying that the register contents did not change.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE if self-test was not successful.

briefdescription.para: Check if the watchdog timer has expired.

detaileddescription.para: This function is used for polled mode and it is also used to check if the last reset was caused by the watchdog timer.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the watchdog has expired.
FALSE if the watchdog has not expired.

detaileddescription.para: An application needs to call this function periodically to keep the timer from asserting the enabled output.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XWdtPs instance.
is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be read

simplesect.para: C-style signature: u32 XWdtPs_ReadReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset)

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device
is the register offset to be written
is the 32-bit value to write to the register

simplesect.para: C-style signature: void XWdtPs_WriteReg(UINTPTR BaseAddress, u32 RegOffset, u32 Data)

briefdescription.para: Contains the implementation of interface functions of the XWdtPs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains method for static initialization (compile-time) of the driver.
Contains diagnostic self-test functions for the XWdtPs driver.
This file contains the hardware interface to the System Watch Dog Timer (WDT).
This file contains a table that specifies the configuration of the watchdog timer devices in the system.
See xwdtps.h for a description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm/jz 01/15/10 First release 1.02a sg 07/15/12 Removed code/APIs related to External Signal Length functionality for CR 658287 Removed APIs XWdtPs_SetExternalSignalLength, XWdtPs_GetExternalSignalLength 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.2 sne 08/05/19 Fixed coverity warnings. 3.6 sb 06/27/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: Each device should have an entry in the table.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm/jz 01/15/10 First release 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm/jz 01/15/10 First release 1.02a sg 07/15/12 Removed defines related to External Signal Length functionality for CR 658287 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm/jz 01/15/10 First release 1.02a sg 08/01/12 Modified it use the Reset Length mask for the self test for CR 658287 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a ecm/jz 01/15/10 First release 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.6 sb 06/27/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 3.9 vlt 12/18/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details. 3.9 vlt 03/14/26 Updated BaseAddress type from u32 to UINTPTR to support 64-bit addressing.
